text.title: Trunk-Based CI Gates Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Pre-commit takes 90 seconds; engineers commit with --no-verify; broken code reaches CI without local check.
antipattern.fix: Remove pytest from pre-commit. Keep ruff + secret scan + format. Run pytest in CI.

antipattern.symptom: Engineers push 5 commits then go to lunch; review queue grows; integration failures emerge on Monday.
antipattern.root-cause: CI wall time > 10 min; rapid feedback is broken.
antipattern.fix: Parallelise pytest into N workers (pytest-xdist); cache dependencies; move slow integration suite to nightly via @pytest.mark.slow + scheduled workflow.

antipattern.symptom: A renamed CI job runs but does not gate merges; PRs with a failing renamed job merge into trunk.
antipattern.root-cause: Required-status list still references the old job name.
antipattern.fix: After any CI rename/add, run gh api repos/<o>/<r>/branches/main/protection and compare list to actual job names. Automate the check in a weekly workflow.

antipattern.symptom: Trunk breaks at 2pm; revert in flight at 4pm because the original author is in another timezone; main blocked for hours.
antipattern.root-cause: Reverts done by humans; fix-forward + 'one more fix' on top further destabilises trunk.
antipattern.fix: Wire auto-revert bot (e.g., probot, custom GH action) that reverts on CI red within 15 minutes; author opens fresh PR with the real fix.

antipattern.symptom: API key committed in PR; nobody noticed; key leaked in public repo for 11 days before rotation.
antipattern.root-cause: No secret-scan hook in pre-commit, no scan in CI.
antipattern.fix: Add detect-secrets + gitleaks to pre-commit; add a CI secret-scan job in required-status list.
